forecast-period.text: A high pressure system south of WA extends a ridge over South Australia. This ridge will persist over SA this weekend as the high moves slowly east to be south of the Bight by tomorrow night. The high is forecast to weaken and step back to the west early next week allowing a trough to pass over the southeast Tuesday night.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Showers about the southern agricultural area and isolated showers about the northern agricultural area, easing and contracting to near southern coasts from late afternoon. Mostly sunny in the north. Maximum temperatures well below average. Southwesterly winds in the south, fresh and gusty near the coast, tending southwest to southeasterly in the north.

Patchy morning fog in the west. Isolated light showers about the agricultural area, mostly about coastal areas. Dry elsewhere. Maximum temperatures well below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds, fresh at times near southern coasts.

Patchy morning fog about the agricultural area. Isolated light showers about the southern agricultural area, mostly about southern coastal fringes, contracting to southern coasts later. Dry elsewhere. Maximum temperatures below average. Light and variable winds in the south, tending moderate southeast to easterly in the north. Afternoon sea breezes near the coast.

text.p: Showers about southern coasts, extending to remaining parts of the southern agricultural area later in the day. Maximum temperatures below average. Mostly light to moderate southeast to southwesterly winds.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Tuesday: 2 to 5 mm about southern agricultural, increasing to 5 to 15 mm about the southern coasts, southern Mount Lofty Ranges, and about northeastern Spencer Gulf region. Less than 2 mm for northern agricultural area and the south of the Pastoral districts.
